channel.title: The Modification Shop
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "The Modification Shop"

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
25Airships, when giving feedback in this forum, try to provide critique that is specific to the concept rather than vague and interchangeable between any post.

What about the concept isn't interesting, and why? What part of the premise needs to be explained better? Is it an issue of needing more detail or writing with better clarity?

For more information on giving feedback in this forum, please see this thread: http://scp-wiki.wikidot.com/forum/t-9804362/so-you-want-to-be-a-concept-reviewer

item.title: Re: The Modification Shop
item.content:encoded: It's an okay idea but not very interesting. Try to explain things a bit better as well.

item.title: Re: The Modification Shop
item.content:encoded:
It sounds like it works too well, we tend to thrive on malfunctions. The invisible force that prevents customers from leaving when they haven't paid may be an interesting hook. Maybe focus on the D-classes that couldn't pay and are stuck inside.

You may want to read SCP-212 which is arguably similar or SCP-2904 which is another SCP that seems to be beneficial until it turns out to have major drawbacks that make it less useful that anticipated. I hope those give you some ideas on what to do with this.

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
Since this thread was on page 6 of the forum listing with no responses, this is a quick staff reminder to the author that if you haven't yet, you should contact reviewers directly to request feedback as needed.

Please refer to this thread for more information: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282692/how-to-get-more-reviews

If you have already reached out to multiple reviewers and received no reply, notify a staff member for assistance finding a reviewer.

item.title: The Modification Shop
item.content:encoded:
Seeking Greenlights: No, I would like feedback.

Page Type: SCP Article

Elevator Pitch: The Modification Shop is a shop that will keep on growing from only Human Modification to cars, guns, and more. The shop is run by a machine and the shop has a invisible barrier that will not allow you to leave if have any not paid for items on you or if you have not paid yet. The things you buy from the shop are modifications that will be used as modules for the system when the subject is placed inside a tube, one of the modules makes the space of the module capacity bigger. It starts at 3 and the module will use one space and will add 3 spaces. The shop will make its own prices as you buy the items and it must be paid in full within 24 hours or the people in the shop at that time will be released and the shop will close for 1 week. The shop earns a safe class as it will do no harm.

Central Narrative: The shop is an ever-growing shop without limits. You buy modules that make modifications to the object you put in the modification room. The shop is run by a robot and if you don’t pay or try to leave with an unpaid item a force will not allow the subject to leave the shop. The prices of the shop's items can be bargained and the foundation usually uses it to generate their military or D-Class. They also are still testing the capabilities of the machine because of the fact it keeps on growing, and that there is an Everything-Bank, The Everything-Bank is a machine that gives points for things you put in depending on the value. There is a Modification card where you store the points, and as in the name, it can eat everything so the foundation uses it as their Rubbish bin while getting free points.

Hook/Attention-Grabber: Need help with this.

Additional Notes:
